title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Thank interest contributing RcppML! document provides guidelines instructions contributing project.

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Fork clone repository: Install dependencies: Build test:
code: git clone https://github.com/zdebruine/RcppML.git cd RcppML install.packages(c("Rcpp", "RcppEigen", "Matrix", "testthat", "devtools", "roxygen2", "knitr", "rmarkdown")) devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Edit files R/ roxygen2 comments (#' @param, #' @export, etc.) Regenerate documentation: Fix Rcpp info bug: Run tests:
code: devtools::document() bash tools/fix_rcpp_info_bug.sh devtools::test()

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: C++ algorithm code lives inst/include/RcppML/ header-library. compiled file src/RcppFunctions.cpp, provides // [[Rcpp::export]] wrappers. changing // [[Rcpp::export]] annotations, run: :
code: devtools::document()  # Also runs Rcpp::compileAttributes() bash tools/fix_rcpp_info_bug.sh

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Follow standard R style (snake_case functions, PascalCase S4 classes) exported functions must complete roxygen documentation including @return @examples Use stop() user-facing errors clear messages

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Namespace: RcppML (PascalCase) new code Templates: use Scalar floating-point type parameter Headers must Rcpp-free — use RCPPML_LOG_INFO() instead Rcpp::Rcout Sparse matrices: expect Eigen::SparseMatrix<Scalar> CSC format Internal W stored transposed (k × m) cache efficiency

title: Contributing to RcppML
code: devtools::test()                                    # All tests testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test_nmf.R")    # Single file

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Place test files tests/testthat/ named test_*.R Use skip_if_not_installed() optional dependencies Use skip_if(Sys.getenv("RCPPML_SKIP_GPU") == "true") GPU tests Ground truth recovery tests use multi-restart robustness

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: GPU tests require CUDA-capable GPU compiled GPU library. HPC cluster:
code: sbatch tests/run_tests_gpu.sbatch

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: run tests benchmarks login node. Use:
code: salloc --partition=cpu --cpus-per-task=8 --mem=32G --time=02:00:00

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Create feature branch: git checkout -b feature/-feature Make changes tests Ensure devtools::check() passes errors Push open Pull Request main

title: Contributing to RcppML
text: Open issue https://github.com/zdebruine/RcppML/issues : - minimal reproducible example - R session info (sessionInfo()) - Expected vs actual behavior

previous_headings: 2. Outstanding Items > 2.2 Significant (Fix Before Release, OK to Defer for CRAN)
what: 2.2.1 NB Dispersion Not Updated in Streaming Path ✅
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed (R-level warning). R/nmf_thin.R warns loss="nb" streaming mode without pre-estimated nb_size. Full C++ dispersion re-estimation deferred. File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppLine: ~205–215 (nb_size_vec initialization) nb_size_vec initialized config.nb_size_init never updated NMF iteration streaming path. -memory NB path (fit_unified.hpp) updates dispersion vector iteration via moment-matching Newton-Raphson. means streaming NB NMF silently uses fixed dispersion — incorrect dispersion estimation needed. Impact: Medium. user must pass pre-estimated nb_size parameter. don’t, results wrong (incorrect bounds — wrong estimates). Fix: complete pass transpose chunks (end W-update fit_chunked.hpp), re-estimate nb_size_vec residuals. requires third pass data per iteration (clever approximation existing two passes’ accumulated statistics). Workaround: Document streaming NB requires nb_size pre-specified. Add warning loss="nb" nb_size provided streaming mode.

previous_headings: 2. Outstanding Items > 2.2 Significant (Fix Before Release, OK to Defer for CRAN)
what: 2.2.2 mask_zeros=FALSE CV Is O(m·n) in Streaming Path ✅
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed. Added holdout_zero_rows() holdout_zero_cols() speckled_cv.hpp using SplitMix64 hash sampling. Added Gram-trick zero-entry branch fit_chunked.hpp. longer O(m) per column. File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppLines: ~332–346, ~384–396 (per-column loop, zero-entry loop) mask_zeros=FALSE (full-matrix CV), streaming path scans m rows every column j find zero entries might holdout: O(m) per column — O(m·n) total per H-update pass, identical dense matrix complexity. defeats point sparse streaming dense CV. Similarly W-update transpose pass. Impact: High large sparse matrices mask_zeros=FALSE. practice mask_zeros=TRUE (sparse CV) natural choice SPZ data (recommendation-style), users won’t hit . Still fixed. Fix: Pre-generate zero-entry holdout set per panel sampling uniformly hash function, without iterating dense. LazySpeckledMask already uses hash — extend enumerate holdout zero entries within column range without scanning rows.
code: for (uint32_t i = 0; i < m; ++i) {     if (ni < nz_rows.size() && nz_rows[ni] == (int)i) { ++ni; continue; }     if (cv_mask->is_holdout(i, gj))         excl_rows.push_back((int)i); }

what: 2.2.3 SVD Init Decompresses Full Matrix During Streaming ✅
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed. Added RAM check (70% threshold) fit_streaming_spz.hpp. Falls back random init warning. platform.hpp provides get_available_ram_bytes() utility. File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_streaming_spz.hppLines: ~115–168 init_mode=1 (Lanczos) init_mode=2 (IRLBA), streaming entry point decompresses entire matrix dense SpMat A_full memory: completely negates memory benefit streaming SVD initialization. matrix requires streaming precisely doesn’t fit memory OOM SVD init. Workarounds exist: - Random init (init_mode=0) avoids — decompression needed. Already default streaming. - code logs: "Building temporary matrix Lanczos init..." user can observe happening. Impact: High SVD init requested matrix doesn’t fit RAM. Low common usage since random init default streaming. Fix: Implement streaming Lanczos power iteration processes data column--column DataLoader, maintaining k Lanczos vectors memory. similar streaming SVD svd/streaming.hpp — check code can reused. streaming SVD already works --core, wire streaming NMF init path. Short-term: Add check: init_mode != 0 file large (heuristic based loader.nnz() available RAM), warn fall back random init.
code: // Decompress all forward chunks into a full sparse matrix SpMat A_full(m, n); // ... fills A_full from chunks ... detail::initialize_lanczos(W_T_svd, H_svd, d_svd, A_full, ...);

previous_headings: 2. Outstanding Items > 2.2 Significant (Fix Before Release, OK to Defer for CRAN)
what: 2.2.4 std::async Per Chunk Creates Thread Per I/O Operation ✅
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed. Replaced PingPongPrefetcher (persistent background thread + 2-slot ping-pong buffer) io/ping_pong_prefetch.hpp. 3 async sites fit_chunked.hpp replaced. Race condition shared write_idx_ found fixed (separate read_idx_ write_target). File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppLines: ~289, ~462, ~493 (std::async(std::launch::async, ...)) double-buffered prefetch creates new thread every single chunk: Spawning thread per chunk non-trivial overhead platforms, future’s .get() full synchronization point. file hundreds chunks, creates hundreds short-lived threads. Better pattern: Use persistent background /O thread dequeues requests work queue, pre-allocated thread pool. current pattern technically works overlaps compute /O since std::launch::async guarantees new thread, inefficient many small chunks. Impact: Marginal large chunk sizes (e.g., 10K columns per chunk) thread startup overhead (~10–50 μs) negligible vs /O time. Matters small chunk sizes fast storage (NVMe /O μs-scale). Fix: Replace double-buffered std::async persistent background thread using std::thread + 2-slot ping-pong buffer std::condition_variable synchronization. See: profiling/stream_timer.hpp example threading pattern.
code: auto prefetch = std::async(std::launch::async, [&loader, &chunk_next]() {     return loader.next_forward(*chunk_next); });

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed. Replaced goto forward_done (has_first) conditional block wrapping forward pass loop. File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppLine: ~248 (goto forward_done;)Line: ~462 (forward_done: label) symmetric NMF path skips entire forward H-update loop goto: code maintainability issue. goto jumps backward code block never entered, confusing read may trigger static analysis warnings. Fix: Wrap else branch function use { } (false) break, restructure lambda. E.g.:
code: if (is_symmetric) {     G_H.noalias() = W_T * W_T.transpose(); } else {     loader.reset_forward();     // ... 200 lines of forward chunk processing ...     goto forward_done;  // <-- symmetric jumps here } ... forward_done: auto run_forward_pass = [&]() { /* ... */ }; if (!is_symmetric) run_forward_pass();

what: 2.2.6 Loss History Reports MSE for IRLS Losses ✅
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Status: Fixed. Added compute_loss() math/loss.hpp nonzero entries IRLS-derived zero-entry loss branch streaming per-column loop. Loss history now reports correct IRLS objective. File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppLines: ~440–463 (Gram-trick loss) ~430–437 (per-column loss) Gram-trick batch path computes train_loss_accum = trAtA - 2*cross_term + recon_norm, squared Frobenius loss. per-column path accumulates diff * diff nonzero entries. MSE approximations. use_irls=true (GP, NB, Gamma, etc.), solver IRLS convergence check uses MSE. means: 1. result.loss_history contains MSE values, GP/NB negative log-likelihood 2. Convergence tolerance checked MSE change, actual IRLS objective Impact: Medium. MSE proxy usually tracks IRLS objective adequately convergence, reported loss values misleading (labeled “loss” actually MSE). test_streaming.R:TEST-STREAM-LOSS-TRACK test verifies decreasing_loss=TRUE, catch loss ’s decreasing MSE terms. Fix: use_irls=true streaming mode, compute per-column IRLS loss (GP log-likelihood, NB log-likelihood, etc.) alongside MSE nonzero entry. already done per-column fit_unified.hpp; port logic streaming path’s per-column loop.

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Correct fundamental structure: forward pass (H-update) transpose pass (W-update) clearly separated double-buffered async chunk reading. right algorithm --core ALS. DataLoader abstraction: fit_chunked.hpp takes generic DataLoader<Scalar>& rather tied SPZ files. means chunked NMF logic works -memory loaders (testing, FactorNet graph layers) streaming loaders (SPZ files). Clean separation /O compute. Memory claims correct: comment Memory: O(m*k + n*k + max_chunk_nnz) accurate. factors W H fully -memory (required ALS — need H computing W-update), one chunk data time materialized. Double-buffered chunk /O: std::async prefetch overlaps /O compute — next chunk decompressed current chunk processed. correct approach hide /O latency. Speckled CV streaming: LazySpeckledMask hash-based holdout works correctly mask_zeros=TRUE (sparse) CV — precomputed mask needed, just hash (row, col, seed). efficient streaming.

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Severity: High SVD init (default -memory NMF) decompresses entire matrix: matrix requires streaming doesn’t fit RAM, OOM occurs . implementation notes random init (init_mode=0) avoids , random init actual default spz_path set. code path exists can triggered init="lanczos" init="irlba". Best practice: --core SVD uses single-pass randomized power iteration (Halko, Martinsson, Tropp 2011) streaming Lanczos. chunk-compatible streaming Lanczos (SpMV rather random access) documented svd/streaming.hpp svd/streaming_matvec.hpp — wired streaming NMF init path make SVD init streaming-safe.
code: // fit_streaming_spz.hpp:~115 SpMat A_full(m, n); // ... fills from all forward chunks ... detail::initialize_lanczos(W_T_svd, H_svd, d_svd, A_full, ...);

previous_headings: 3. Streaming Out-of-Core: Architecture Critique > 3.2 Critical Flaws
what: 3.2.2 NB dispersion parameter is fixed in streaming
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Severity: High correctness; Medium practical impact nb_size_vec (NB inverse dispersion r) set start: never updated streaming NMF iteration. -memory NB path re-estimates dispersion residuals iteration (iterations). Streaming NB therefore uses fixed dispersion throughout, equivalent specifying known dispersion upfront — reasonable assumption user passes nb_size, silently wrong default nb_size_init=1 used data different overdispersion. Best practice: Either () implement streaming dispersion rescanning pass end iteration (adds third pass, dispersion estimation converges quickly), (b) document clearly streaming NB requires user-supplied nb_size warn loudly (warning("Streaming NB uses fixed dispersion...")) default used.
code: nb_size_vec = DenseVector<Scalar>::Constant(m, config.nb_size_init);

previous_headings: 3. Streaming Out-of-Core: Architecture Critique > 3.2 Critical Flaws
what: 3.2.3 mask_zeros=FALSE CV is O(m·n) per iteration
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Severity: High large matrices per-column H-update W-update loops, finding holdout zero entries requires scanning m rows (n columns W-update): 100K×200K matrix, 100K iterations × 200K columns = 2×10¹⁰ operations per H-update pass — indistinguishable dense complexity. mask_zeros=TRUE (default sparse data) avoids examining existing nonzeros. mask_zeros=FALSE option dense CV explicitly supported. Best practice: Reformulate LazySpeckledMask support range-based enumeration: given column j row range [0, m), return holdout rows using hash function directly without iterating m rows. Since mask uses uniform hash (hold fraction f), expected number holdout entries per column f·m — can generated hashing candidate rows rather testing (e.g., using inverse hash sampling pre-sampled Poisson draws per column).
code: for (uint32_t i = 0; i < m; ++i) {            // O(m) per column     if (nz_rows[ni] == (int)i) { ++ni; continue; }     if (cv_mask->is_holdout(i, gj)) excl_rows.push_back((int)i); }

previous_headings: 3. Streaming Out-of-Core: Architecture Critique > 3.3 Moderate Issues
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: std::async(std::launch::async, ...) call inner chunk loop spawns joins thread chunk: fast NVMe storage SPZ decompression bottleneck (disk /O), works well compute decompression run concurrently. network file systems (NFS — common HPC) /O latency high, spawning threads per-chunk adds overhead. Better: persistent producer thread maintains ring buffer 2–4 pre-decompressed chunks. consumer (main thread) simply picks next ready chunk. eliminates thread spawn/join overhead can hold pre-fetched chunks.
code: auto prefetch = std::async(std::launch::async, [&loader, chunk_next]() {     return loader.next_forward(*chunk_next); }); // ... compute ... bool has_next = prefetch.get();  // blocks until I/O done

previous_headings: 6. StreamPress Architecture Revision Plan > 6.0 Context and Motivation
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Critical loader bug: SpzLoader DenseSpzLoader read entire file RAM constructors (file_data_.resize(file_size_);   fread(...)) — completely defeats --core streaming. 50 GB .spz file requires 50 GB RAM just open . correctness bug, performance issue. Dense v3 compression: Raw float32/float64 bytes . 48 reserved bytes FileHeader_v3 unused can carry codec metadata. Chunk size 256 severely sub-optimal: DEFAULT_CHUNK_COLS=256 causes hundreds thousands PCIe transfers kernel launches per epoch GPU. optimal range modern hardware 2048–4096+ columns per chunk. Streaming /O dominates low k: benchmarks/stream_opt/STREAMING_ANALYSIS.md, /O accounts 71–73% total time k=8. Chunk size directly controls overhead. /proc/meminfo Windows: Memory detection logic must platform-conditional. Laptop users (Windows, macOS, 8–16 GB RAM) important target — auto-dispatch must handle low-memory environments safely.

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Files: inst/include/sparsepress/format/header_v3.hpp, inst/include/sparsepress/sparsepress_v3.hpp dense v3 format 48 reserved bytes FileHeader_v3.reserved[48]. Use first two bytes codec metadata: existing v3 files written reserved[0]=0 (zero-filled default), read RAW_FP32 — automatically backwards compatible. new version number needed.
code: // reserved[0]: compression codec enum DenseCodec : uint8_t {     RAW_FP32   = 0,  // current format — no change, fully backwards compatible     FP16       = 1,  // lossless fp16 truncation (50% size)     QUANT8     = 2,  // 8-bit quantization (75% size, ~0.4% error)     FP16_RANS  = 3,  // fp16 + XOR-delta columns + rANS entropy (best ratio)     FP32_RANS  = 4,  // fp32 + XOR-delta columns + rANS entropy }; // reserved[1]: delta encoding flag (0=none, 1=XOR-delta between adjacent columns)

code: float32 panel → fp16_convert (value_map.hpp) → XOR-delta transform               → rANS encode (rans.hpp) → compressed bytes → DenseChunkDescriptor

previous_headings: 6. StreamPress Architecture Revision Plan > 6.4 Phase 3: User-Configurable Chunk Size (Default 2048)
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: laptop 8 GB RAM m=30K genes, gives ~2,700 columns/chunk. 256 GB HPC node, ~85,000 columns/chunk — whole matrix one chunk, falling back -core mode automatically.
code: uint32_t choose_chunk_cols(uint64_t m, uint32_t k, uint64_t ram_avail_bytes) {     // Target: one chunk decompressed ≈ 1% of available memory budget     // (ensures ~100 chunks can be kept in memory simultaneously during transpose)     uint64_t bytes_per_col = m * sizeof(float);     uint64_t target_chunk_bytes = ram_avail_bytes / 100;     uint32_t auto_cols = (uint32_t)(target_chunk_bytes / bytes_per_col);     return std::clamp(auto_cols, (uint32_t)256, (uint32_t)32768); }

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: Two complementary modes: write (include_transpose=TRUE): streaming x chunk chunk (col-major), accumulate sorted-row buffer transpose section. Working memory O(m × chunk_cols × val_bytes) — one forward chunk. second pass required. transpose section appended file; FileHeader offsets patched close. Post-hoc (st_add_transpose()): existing .spz files lack transpose section. Uses streaming sort algorithm tmpdir intermediate chunk buffers. Memory requirement: O(chunk_cols × m × val_bytes) time. modes produce identical -disk result. forward transpose sections independently addressable via FileHeader offsets, SpzLoader::next_forward() SpzLoader::next_transpose() continue work unchanged.
code: st_write(x, path, include_transpose = TRUE) st_add_transpose(path, tmpdir = tempdir(), verbose = TRUE)

title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit

previous_headings: 6. StreamPress Architecture Revision Plan > 6.7 Phase 6: Memory-Aware Auto-Dispatch
title: RcppML / FactorNet — Production Audit
text: threshold decision: safety_margin=0.70 decompression_ratio read file header (fp16≈2.0, raw≈1.0, rANS≈0.5—2.0).
code: IN_CORE_GPU    matrix fits in GPU VRAM → load all to device, run in-core GPU NMF CPU_TO_GPU     fits in CPU RAM but not VRAM → pin to CPU RAM, stream chunks to GPU STREAMING_GPU  matrix too large for CPU RAM → I/O→CPU→GPU pipeline (1 chunk at a time) IN_CORE_CPU    matrix fits in CPU RAM, no GPU → load all, run in-core CPU NMF STREAMING_CPU  matrix too large for CPU RAM, no GPU → streaming CPU NMF compressed_file_size × decompression_ratio < available × safety_margin

title: WORKQUEUE_2 — Streaming Algorithm Bug Fixes & Performance
text: File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppContext: mask_zeros=FALSE, finding holdout zero entries requires iterating m rows per column — O(m·n) total, defeating sparse streaming. Locate loop: Search fit_chunked.hpp loop iterates (uint32_t = 0; < m; ++) CV path checks cv_mask->is_holdout(, gj) zero entries nz_rows[ni] != . Fix strategy: Add method LazySpeckledMask (inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/speckled_cv.hpp wherever defined) generates holdout rows given column directly hash, without iterating m rows: implementation iterates expected ~f·m candidates using inverse hash function (seeded PRNG replicates is_holdout() return). changes O(m) per column O(f·m). hash-inverse approach complex, pragmatic middle ground: pre-generate sorted list holdout_zero_rows[col] LazySpeckledMask construction chunk range, stored std::unordered_map<int, std::vector<int>>. trades memory scan time. Either way, final test: synthetic matrix m=50000, mask_zeros=FALSE, confirm CV still produces reasonable test error (correctness), measure whether wall time now sublinear m*n.
code: // New method on LazySpeckledMask (or a free function): // Returns a vector of holdout row indices for this column WITHOUT scanning all rows. // Uses the same hash: hash(row, col, seed) % inv_density == 0 // Strategy: instead of testing all i in [0,m), use the observation that for // a holdout fraction f = 1/inv_density, the expected count is f*m. // Generate candidates by seeded random sampling rather than full scan. std::vector<int> holdout_zeros_in_col(int col, int m,                                        const std::vector<int>& nz_rows) const;

code: // Estimate decompressed size in bytes: uint64_t est_bytes = (uint64_t)m * (uint64_t)n * sizeof(Scalar);  // Query available RAM: uint64_t avail_bytes = get_available_ram_bytes();  // see below  if (init_mode != 0 && est_bytes > avail_bytes * 0.70) {     // Warn and fall back     Rcpp::warning(         "SVD initialization requires decompressing the full matrix (%s GB), "         "but only %s GB RAM is available. Falling back to random initialization. "         "Pass init=\"random\" to suppress this warning.",         to_gb_str(est_bytes), to_gb_str(avail_bytes)     );     init_mode = 0;  // random } inline uint64_t get_available_ram_bytes() { #if defined(_WIN32)     MEMORYSTATUSEX status;     status.dwLength = sizeof(status);     GlobalMemoryStatusEx(&status);     return (uint64_t)status.ullAvailPhys; #elif defined(__APPLE__)     // macOS: sysctl vm.page_free_count * page_size     int64_t page_size = sysconf(_SC_PAGE_SIZE);     int64_t free_pages = 0;     size_t len = sizeof(free_pages);     sysctlbyname("vm.page_free_count", &free_pages, &len, nullptr, 0);     return (uint64_t)(page_size * free_pages); #else     // Linux: read MemAvailable from /proc/meminfo     std::ifstream f("/proc/meminfo");     std::string line;     while (std::getline(f, line)) {         if (line.rfind("MemAvailable:", 0) == 0) {             uint64_t kb = 0;             sscanf(line.c_str(), "MemAvailable: %llu kB", &kb);             return kb * 1024ULL;         }     }     return 0;  // unknown — caller should treat as "insufficient" #endif }

what: Task 4 — Replace std::async Per-Chunk with Persistent Thread (§2.2.4)
title: WORKQUEUE_2 — Streaming Algorithm Bug Fixes & Performance
text: File: inst/include/FactorNet/nmf/fit_chunked.hppContext: current prefetch pattern spawns joins new OS thread per chunk: works non-trivial overhead (~10–50 µs per thread creation) wasted fast NVMe storage. Fix: Replace persistent 2-slot ping-pong buffer using dedicated background /O thread. well-known pattern: background thread calls load_fn_(slots_[next_slot]) main thread processes slots_[current_]. Replace forward pass transpose pass std::async calls abstraction. implementation standalone header inst/include/FactorNet/io/ping_pong_prefetch.hpp can also used directly GPU streaming path. replacement, run streaming tests confirm regression check wall time better: Note: std::thread requires linking -lpthread. RcppML already link via SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION. Check DESCRIPTION : add -lpthread src/Makevars thread tests fail link:
code: auto prefetch = std::async(std::launch::async, [&loader, &chunk_next]() {     return loader.next_forward(*chunk_next); }); // ... compute ... bool has_next = prefetch.get(); // PingPongPrefetcher<ChunkType>: manages a background thread that pre-loads // the next chunk while the current chunk is being processed. template<typename ChunkType> class PingPongPrefetcher {     std::array<ChunkType, 2> slots_;     int current_ = 0;     std::thread worker_;     std::mutex mu_;     std::condition_variable cv_ready_, cv_free_;     bool slot_ready_[2] = {false, false};     bool slot_free_[2] = {true, true};     bool done_ = false;     std::function<bool(ChunkType&)> load_fn_; public:     PingPongPrefetcher(std::function<bool(ChunkType&)> load_fn);     ~PingPongPrefetcher();  // signals done, joins worker_     bool get_next(ChunkType& out);  // blocks until next chunk is ready }; ssh c001 'cd /mnt/home/debruinz/RcppML-2 && module load r/4.5.2 && OMP_NUM_THREADS=4 Rscript -e "   library(RcppML)   data(monocle3_subset)   f <- tempfile(fileext=\".spz\")   sp_write(monocle3_subset, f)   system.time(nmf(f, k=8, maxit=5))   unlink(f) "' SystemRequirements: C++14, OpenMP PKG_LIBS += -lpthread

title: WORKQUEUE_2 — Streaming Algorithm Bug Fixes & Performance
text: File: R/nmf_thin.RContext: solver="auto" currently maps solver_mode=0L (CD) via switch default line ~605: Fix: Implement advertised auto-selection logic. Empirically: - Cholesky faster small k (k < 32) MSE loss L1 regularization - CD required L1 > 0 (Cholesky enforce L1 sparsity) - CD better large k (k ≥ 32) IRLS losses Replace switch : Find exact variable names l1_w l1_h nmf_thin.R — may called l1 l1_w/l1_h depending function signature. Also find loss normalized (may called distribution loss_type point function). Add test solver="auto" behavior tests/testthat/test_solver.R (create doesn’t exist) add existing solver test file: solver_mode value needs accessible diagnostics — check ’s already result@diagnostics add .
code: solver_mode <- switch(solver, cd = 0L, cholesky = 1L, 0L) if (solver == "auto") {   # Cholesky+clip is faster for small k with MSE and no L1 regularization   if (k < 32 && loss == "mse" && l1_w == 0 && l1_h == 0) {     solver_mode <- 1L  # Cholesky   } else {     solver_mode <- 0L  # CD   } } else {   solver_mode <- switch(solver, cd = 0L, cholesky = 1L, 0L) } test_that("solver='auto' selects Cholesky for small k MSE", {   res <- nmf(monocle3_subset, k=8, solver="auto", loss="mse")   expect_equal(res@diagnostics$solver_mode, 1L)  # 1 = Cholesky }) test_that("solver='auto' selects CD when L1 > 0", {   res <- nmf(monocle3_subset, k=8, solver="auto", loss="mse", L1=0.1)   expect_equal(res@diagnostics$solver_mode, 0L)  # 0 = CD })

what: Step 3c: Add choose_chunk_cols() helper (optional, used by auto-dispatch)
title: WORKQUEUE_3 — StreamPress Architecture Revision
text: Create inst/include/FactorNet/io/chunk_size.hpp:
code: #pragma once #include <cstdint> #include <algorithm>  namespace streampress {  /// Heuristic chunk column count for streaming NMF. /// Targets ~1% of available RAM per chunk. inline uint32_t choose_chunk_cols(uint64_t m, uint64_t ram_avail_bytes) {     if (ram_avail_bytes == 0) return 2048;     uint64_t bytes_per_col = m * sizeof(float);     uint64_t target_bytes = ram_avail_bytes / 100;     uint64_t auto_cols = target_bytes / bytes_per_col;     return (uint32_t)std::clamp(auto_cols, (uint64_t)256, (uint64_t)32768); }  } // namespace streampress

title: WORKQUEUE_3 — StreamPress Architecture Revision
text: Add R/streampress.R (new R/dispatch.R):
code: .st_dispatch <- function(path, k, resource = "auto") {   info <- st_info(path)   file_size <- file.info(path)$size      # Estimate decompressed size: depends on codec in file header   # For v2 sparse: compressed_file_size * ~8 (typical 8x compression on scRNA)   # For v3 dense: check codec field; raw = 1x, fp16 = ~2x   decomp_factor <- if (info$version == 2L) 8.0 else 1.0   est_decomp_bytes <- file_size * decomp_factor      # Available RAM   avail_ram <- .get_available_ram_bytes()  # calls C++ get_available_ram_bytes()      # Available VRAM (0 if no GPU or GPU not requested)   avail_vram <- if (resource %in% c("gpu", "auto") && isTRUE(.gpu_available())) {     .get_available_vram_bytes()   } else 0L      safety <- 0.70      if (avail_vram > 0 && est_decomp_bytes < avail_vram * safety) {     list(mode = "IN_CORE_GPU", resource = "gpu", streaming = FALSE)   } else if (avail_vram > 0 && est_decomp_bytes < avail_ram * safety) {     list(mode = "CPU_TO_GPU", resource = "gpu", streaming = FALSE)     } else if (avail_vram > 0) {     list(mode = "STREAMING_GPU", resource = "gpu", streaming = TRUE)   } else if (est_decomp_bytes < avail_ram * safety) {     list(mode = "IN_CORE_CPU", resource = "cpu", streaming = FALSE)   } else {     list(mode = "STREAMING_CPU", resource = "cpu", streaming = TRUE)   } }

if (is.character(data) && grepl("\.spz$", data, ignore.case = TRUE)) {   if (is.null(dispatch) || dispatch == "auto") {     mode_info <- .st_dispatch(data, k, resource = resource)     if (verbose) message("StreamPress auto-dispatch: ", mode_info$mode)     resource <- mode_info$resource     streaming <- mode_info$streaming   } else {     # Manual override — fire a non-suppressable warning     message(       "WARNING: `dispatch` is set manually to '", dispatch, "'.
",       "  Auto-dispatch ensures sufficient RAM is available before loading.
",       "  Manual dispatch may cause out-of-memory errors or crashes.
",       "  Remove `dispatch=` to restore safe automatic mode."     )   } }

#!/usr/bin/env Rscript # Verifies that all .spz files in the GEO reprocessed corpus are readable # after the StreamPress rename. # # Usage: Rscript tools/verify_spz_corpus.R [corpus_dir] [n_sample]  library(RcppML)  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE) corpus_dir <- if (length(args) >= 1) args[1] else {   stop("Usage: Rscript tools/verify_spz_corpus.R <corpus_dir> [n_sample]") } n_sample <- if (length(args) >= 2) as.integer(args[2]) else 100L  files <- list.files(corpus_dir, pattern = "\.spz$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE) cat(sprintf("Found %d .spz files in %s
", length(files), corpus_dir))  sample_files <- sample(files, min(n_sample, length(files))) n_ok <- 0L n_fail <- 0L for (f in sample_files) {   tryCatch({     info <- st_info(f)     stopifnot(info$version == 2L, info$m > 0, info$n > 0, info$nnz > 0)     n_ok <- n_ok + 1L   }, error = function(e) {     cat(sprintf("FAIL: %s — %s
", f, conditionMessage(e)))     n_fail <<- n_fail + 1L   }) } cat(sprintf("Passed: %d / %d sampled files
", n_ok, n_ok + n_fail)) if (n_fail > 0) stop(sprintf("%d files failed compatibility check!", n_fail)) cat("All sampled files are compatible.
") ssh c001 'cd /mnt/home/debruinz/RcppML-2 && module load r/4.5.2 && R --no-save -e "   devtools::install(quick=TRUE) " && Rscript tools/verify_spz_corpus.R /mnt/projects/debruinz_project/PATH_FROM_PHASE0 100'

code: library(testthat) library(RcppML) library(Matrix)  test_that("st_write/st_read round-trip: sparse matrix", {   set.seed(42)   A <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(200, 100, density = 0.05, repr = "C")   f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz")   on.exit(unlink(f))   st_write(A, f)   B <- st_read(f)   expect_equal(as.matrix(A), as.matrix(B), tolerance = 1e-6) })  test_that("deprecated sp_write still works (but warns)", {   A <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(50, 50, density = 0.1, repr = "C")   f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz")   on.exit(unlink(f))   expect_warning(sp_write(A, f), "deprecated")   B <- sp_read(f)   expect_equal(as.matrix(A), as.matrix(B), tolerance = 1e-6) })  test_that("st_info returns correct dimensions for v2 file", {   A <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(300, 150, density = 0.03, repr = "C")   f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz")   on.exit(unlink(f))   st_write(A, f)   info <- st_info(f)   expect_equal(info$m, 300L)   expect_equal(info$n, 150L)   expect_equal(info$version, 2L) })  test_that("nmf from .spz file works end-to-end", {   A <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(500, 200, density = 0.05, repr = "C")   f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz")   on.exit(unlink(f))   st_write(A, f, include_transpose = TRUE)   res <- nmf(f, k = 4, maxit = 5, tol = 1e-3)   expect_s4_class(res, "nmf")   expect_equal(nrow(res@w), 500L)   expect_equal(ncol(res@h), 200L) })  test_that("chunk_cols parameter accepted by st_write", {   A <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(100, 200, density = 0.1, repr = "C")   f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz")   on.exit(unlink(f))   expect_no_error(st_write(A, f, chunk_cols = 64L))   info <- st_info(f)   expect_equal(info$m, 100L) })

text: Create benchmarks/harness/streaming_spz/bench_geo_k64.R:
code:
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript # Flagship benchmark: GEO single-cell dataset, k=64, NB loss, no manual configuration. # Run on a compute node with: Rscript benchmarks/harness/streaming_spz/bench_geo_k64.R <path.spz> # # Records: wall time, per-iteration breakdown, peak RAM, dispatch mode.  library(RcppML) library(bench)  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE) if (length(args) < 1) stop("Usage: Rscript bench_geo_k64.R <path.spz>") spz_path <- args[1]  stopifnot(file.exists(spz_path))  cat("System info:
") cat("  Node:", Sys.info()["nodename"], "
") cat("  R version:", R.version$version.string, "
") cat("  OMP_NUM_THREADS:", Sys.getenv("OMP_NUM_THREADS", "not set"), "
") cat("  GPU available:", RcppML::gpu_available(), "
") cat("  Available RAM (MB):", round(RcppML:::.get_available_ram_mb(), 1), "

")  info <- st_info(spz_path) cat(sprintf("Dataset: %d genes x %d cells, nnz=%d (%.1f%% density)
",             info$m, info$n, info$nnz,             100 * info$nnz / (info$m * info$n))) cat("File size:", round(file.info(spz_path)$size / 1e9, 2), "GB

")  cat("Running nmf(k=64, loss='nb', maxit=20) ...
") t0 <- proc.time() res <- nmf(   spz_path,   k     = 64,   loss  = "nb",   maxit = 20,   tol   = 1e-4,   verbose = TRUE ) elapsed <- proc.time() - t0  cat(sprintf("
Total wall time: %.1f seconds (%.1f min)
",             elapsed["elapsed"], elapsed["elapsed"] / 60)) cat(sprintf("Final loss: %.4f
", tail(res@loss_history, 1))) cat(sprintf("Dispatch mode: %s
", attr(res, "dispatch_mode") %||% "unknown"))  # Save results results <- list(   spz_path    = spz_path,   info        = info,   k           = 64L,   loss        = "nb",   maxit       = 20L,   elapsed_sec = as.numeric(elapsed["elapsed"]),   loss_history = res@loss_history,   sys_info    = as.list(Sys.info()),   timestamp   = Sys.time() ) out_rds <- file.path("benchmarks/results",                      paste0("geo_k64_", format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"), ".rds")) dir.create("benchmarks/results", showWarnings = FALSE) saveRDS(results, out_rds) cat("Results saved to:", out_rds, "
")

text: FileHeader_v2 uint8_t reserved[32] bytes 96–127 (zero-filled old files). new design repurposes bytes typed fields read accessor methods — raw struct layout unchanged: old files reserved = -zeros: - obs_table_offset == 0 → obs table → st_read_obs() returns empty data.frame - var_table_offset == 0 → var table → st_read_var() returns empty data.frame - transp_chunk_cols == 0 → use chunk_cols transpose - transpose_offset == 0 → transpose stored → st_slice_rows() returns error message: “File transpose section; re-write include_transpose=TRUE” GEO reprocessing jobs currently running cluster generate v2 files include_transpose=FALSE (old write default). files remain fully readable st_read() st_slice_cols(). New writes default include_transpose=TRUE. remove include_transpose parameter. Add accessor methods FileHeader_v2 header_v2.hpp (existing valid() method): changes header_v2.hpp. modify struct sizes, field names, existing methods.
code: reserved[0:7]   → obs_table_offset  (uint64_t, 0 = no obs table) reserved[8:15]  → var_table_offset  (uint64_t, 0 = no var table) reserved[16:19] → transp_chunk_cols (uint32_t, 0 = same as chunk_cols) reserved[20]    → flags             (uint8_t,  bit 0 = has_dimnames) reserved[21:31] → zero (future) // Accessors for reserved-byte extension fields (backwards-compatible read) uint64_t obs_table_offset() const {     uint64_t v = 0; std::memcpy(&v, reserved,     8); return v; } uint64_t var_table_offset() const {     uint64_t v = 0; std::memcpy(&v, reserved + 8, 8); return v; } uint32_t transp_chunk_cols() const {     // 0 means "same as chunk_cols"     uint32_t v = 0; std::memcpy(&v, reserved + 16, 4); return v; } uint8_t flags() const { return reserved[20]; } bool has_dimnames() const { return (flags() & 0x01) != 0; }  void set_obs_table_offset(uint64_t off) { std::memcpy(reserved,      &off, 8); } void set_var_table_offset(uint64_t off) { std::memcpy(reserved + 8,  &off, 8); } void set_transp_chunk_cols(uint32_t cc) { std::memcpy(reserved + 16, &cc,  4); } void set_has_dimnames(bool v) {     reserved[20] = v ? (reserved[20] | 0x01) : (reserved[20] & ~0x01); }

text: Define binary column-store format embedded obs var tables. Provide C++ functions header companion implementation file (obs_var_table_impl.hpp): serializer deserializer must fully correct round-trip lossless. Use pread/ReadFile (platform-appropriate) fseek/fread positional reads.
code: // Sentinel values for missing data static constexpr int32_t  NA_INT32   = INT32_MIN; static constexpr uint32_t NA_UINT32  = UINT32_MAX; static constexpr float    NA_FLOAT32 = std::numeric_limits<float>::quiet_NaN(); static constexpr double   NA_FLOAT64 = std::numeric_limits<double>::quiet_NaN();  enum class ColType : uint8_t {     INT32       = 0,   // raw int32_t array; NA = INT32_MIN     FLOAT32     = 1,   // raw float array;   NA = NaN     FLOAT64     = 2,   // raw double array;  NA = NaN     BOOL        = 3,   // raw uint8_t array (0/1); NA = 255     UINT32      = 4,   // raw uint32_t array; NA = UINT32_MAX     STRING_DICT = 5,   // uint32_t code array (NA = UINT32_MAX) + null-delimited dictionary };  // Fixed-size column descriptor: 112 bytes struct ColDescriptor {     char     name[64];         // null-terminated column name; 63 chars max     uint8_t  col_type;         // ColType enum     uint8_t  nullable;         // 1 if column contains NA sentinels     uint8_t  _pad[2];          // alignment     uint32_t dict_bytes;       // byte count of null-delimited dict (STRING_DICT only; 0 otherwise)     uint64_t data_offset;      // byte offset from start of table blob to raw data array     uint64_t dict_offset;      // byte offset from start of table blob to dictionary blob     uint8_t  _reserved[20];    // zero, future use     // Total: 64+1+1+2+4+8+8+20 = 108 → pad to 112     uint8_t  _align[4]; }; // static_assert(sizeof(ColDescriptor) == 112, "ColDescriptor must be 112 bytes");  // Table header: variable length // Layout in file: [ObsVarTableHeader][ColDescriptor × n_cols][raw data blobs] struct ObsVarTableHeader {     uint8_t  magic[4];     // "OVTB"     uint32_t n_rows;       // number of rows (cells or genes)     uint32_t n_cols;       // number of data columns     uint32_t header_bytes; // byte size of this header + all descriptors     // ColDescriptor[n_cols] follows immediately }; // static_assert(sizeof(ObsVarTableHeader) == 16, "ObsVarTableHeader must be 16 bytes"); // Write an obs/var table to a byte buffer (returned as std::vector<uint8_t>) // col_names: name of each column // col_types: ColType for each column // int_cols:  map of column index → std::vector<int32_t> // flt_cols:  map of column index → std::vector<float> // dbl_cols:  map of column index → std::vector<double> // bool_cols: map of column index → std::vector<uint8_t> // dict_cols: map of column index → pair<std::vector<uint32_t>,string_dict> std::vector<uint8_t> obs_var_table_serialize(     const std::vector<std::string>& col_names,     const std::vector<ColType>&     col_types,     ...);  // Read back to per-column vectors void obs_var_table_deserialize(     const uint8_t* buf,     size_t buf_bytes,     std::vector<std::string>& col_names_out,     std::vector<ColType>&     col_types_out,     ...);

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: Rcpp_sp_read Rcpp_sp_read_transpose decode chunks parallel: - Read chunk descriptors first - Parallel decode: thread decodes assigned chunks independently - pread (Linux/macOS) / ReadFile OVERLAPPED (Windows) thread-safe concurrent reads without seeking (mutexes needed) - Accumulate pre-allocated output arrays Windows note: Use #ifdef _WIN32 guards around pread vs. ReadFile. Provide safe_pread() inline wrapper new file inst/include/streampress/core/platform_io.hpp abstracts . Use safe_pread everywhere reader. gives Linux, macOS, Windows support without platform-specific code paths business logic.
code: // inst/include/streampress/core/platform_io.hpp #ifdef _WIN32 #  include <windows.h> static inline ssize_t safe_pread(int fd, void* buf, size_t count, uint64_t offset) {     HANDLE h = (HANDLE)_get_osfhandle(fd);     OVERLAPPED ov = {};     ov.Offset     = (DWORD)(offset & 0xFFFFFFFFULL);     ov.OffsetHigh = (DWORD)(offset >> 32);     DWORD nread = 0;     if (!ReadFile(h, buf, (DWORD)count, &nread, &ov)) return -1;     return (ssize_t)nread; } #else #  include <unistd.h> static inline ssize_t safe_pread(int fd, void* buf, size_t count, uint64_t offset) {     return pread(fd, buf, count, (off_t)offset); } #endif

text: Algorithm: 1. Open file, read header 2. Check hdr.transpose_offset != 0. 0, throw: “transpose section file. Re-write st_write(…, include_transpose=TRUE).” 3. Read transpose chunk index (stored transpose_offset) 4. Map requested rows → transpose chunks contain (transpose chunk covers transp_chunk_cols rows Aᵀ = columns Aᵀ) 5. Parallel decode needed transpose chunks using safe_pread 6. Extract requested rows decoded columns, assemble output dgCMatrix (rows output = requested rows, cols output = n columns) Return value: dgCMatrix size length(rows) × n. nnz > 2^31 - 1, split list dgCMatrix along columns return R list attribute "col_starts" giving starting column element.

code: #' @export st_slice <- function(path, rows = NULL, cols = NULL, threads = 0L) {   path <- normalizePath(path, mustWork = TRUE)   if (!is.null(rows) && !is.null(cols)) {     # First slice columns (fast), then filter rows from result in memory     A <- st_slice_cols(path, cols, threads = threads)     return(A[rows, , drop = FALSE])   } else if (!is.null(rows)) {     return(st_slice_rows(path, rows, threads = threads))   } else if (!is.null(cols)) {     return(st_slice_cols(path, cols, threads = threads))   } else {     return(st_read(path, threads = threads))   } }

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: new C++ code needed — uses existing st_info().
code: #' Return the column ranges for each chunk in a .spz file #' #' @param path Path to a .spz file. #' @return A data.frame with columns \code{start} and \code{end} (1-indexed, #'   inclusive), one row per chunk. #' @export st_chunk_ranges <- function(path) {   info <- st_info(path)   chunk_cols <- info$chunk_cols   n <- info$cols   starts <- seq(1L, n, by = chunk_cols)   ends   <- pmin(starts + chunk_cols - 1L, n)   data.frame(start = starts, end = ends) }

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: parallel application function every chunk:
code: #' Apply a function to every chunk in a .spz file #' #' @param path Path to a .spz file. #' @param fn Function taking \code{(chunk, col_start, col_end)} where #'   \code{chunk} is a \code{dgCMatrix}. #' @param transpose Logical; if TRUE, iterate over transpose chunks (row chunks #'   of the original matrix). Requires transpose section in file. #' @param threads Integer; decode threads per chunk. Default 0 (all threads). #' @return Invisible list of results from \code{fn}. #' @export st_map_chunks <- function(path, fn, transpose = FALSE, threads = 0L) {   ranges <- st_chunk_ranges(path)   results <- vector("list", nrow(ranges))   if (!transpose) {     for (i in seq_len(nrow(ranges))) {       chunk <- st_slice_cols(path, ranges$start[i]:ranges$end[i], threads = threads)       results[[i]] <- fn(chunk, ranges$start[i], ranges$end[i])     }   } else {     info <- st_info(path)     tc <- if (info$transp_chunk_cols > 0L) info$transp_chunk_cols else info$chunk_cols     row_starts <- seq(1L, info$rows, by = tc)     row_ends   <- pmin(row_starts + tc - 1L, info$rows)     results <- vector("list", length(row_starts))     for (i in seq_along(row_starts)) {       chunk <- st_slice_rows(path, row_starts[i]:row_ends[i], threads = threads)       results[[i]] <- fn(chunk, row_starts[i], row_ends[i])     }   }   invisible(results) }

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: Convenience wrappers read obs/var, filter, slice:
code: #' Read obs metadata, apply filter expression, return matching row indices #' @param path Path to .spz file #' @param ... Named filter criteria passed to \code{subset()} on the obs table #' @return Integer vector of matching row indices (1-based) #' @export st_obs_indices <- function(path, ...) {   obs <- st_read_obs(path)   if (nrow(obs) == 0L) stop("File has no obs table")   matched <- subset(obs, ...)   which(rownames(obs) %in% rownames(matched)) }  #' Slice rows matching obs filter criteria #' @param path Path to .spz file #' @param ... Filter expression on obs columns (passed to subset()) #' @param threads Integer decode threads. 0 = all. #' @return dgCMatrix #' @export st_filter_rows <- function(path, ..., threads = 0L) {   idx <- st_obs_indices(path, ...)   if (length(idx) == 0L) stop("No rows match filter criteria")   st_slice_rows(path, idx, threads = threads) }  #' Slice columns matching var filter criteria #' @param path Path to .spz file #' @param ... Filter expression on var columns (passed to subset()) #' @param threads Integer decode threads. 0 = all. #' @return dgCMatrix #' @export st_filter_cols <- function(path, ..., threads = 0L) {   var <- st_read_var(path)   if (nrow(var) == 0L) stop("File has no var table")   matched <- subset(var, ...)   idx <- which(rownames(var) %in% rownames(matched))   if (length(idx) == 0L) stop("No columns match filter criteria")   st_slice_cols(path, idx, threads = threads) }

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: Allow streaming assembly multiple matrices one .spz: large lists cbinding exhausts RAM, streaming writer ideal complex. now .call(cbind, mats) acceptable; document RAM requirement leave code comment noting future streaming version.
code: #' Write a list of matrices as a single column-concatenated .spz file #' #' Matrices are cbind-assembled. All matrices must have the same number of rows. #' An obs/var table may be provided for the combined matrix. #' #' @param x A list of dgCMatrix objects (or objects coercible to dgCMatrix). #'   All must have identical \code{nrow}. #' @param path Output .spz path. #' @param obs data.frame with cell metadata (nrow == total columns across all x). #' @param var data.frame with gene metadata (nrow == nrow of each matrix in x). #' @param chunk_bytes Target bytes per chunk. Default 64 MB. #' @param chunk_cols Explicit column count per chunk. Overrides chunk_bytes. #' @param include_transpose Logical. Default TRUE. #' @param precision Value precision. Default "auto". #' @param threads Integer. 0 = all threads. #' @param verbose Logical. #' @return Invisibly, a list of compression statistics. #' @export st_write_list <- function(x, path, obs = NULL, var = NULL,                           chunk_bytes = 64e6, chunk_cols = NULL,                           include_transpose = TRUE, precision = "auto",                           threads = 0L, verbose = FALSE) {   stopifnot(is.list(x), length(x) >= 1L)   mats <- lapply(x, function(m) {     if (!inherits(m, "dgCMatrix")) m <- as(m, "dgCMatrix")     m   })   nr <- nrow(mats[[1]])   if (!all(sapply(mats, nrow) == nr)) stop("All matrices must have the same nrow")   combined <- do.call(cbind, mats)   st_write(combined, path, obs = obs, var = var,            chunk_bytes = chunk_bytes, chunk_cols = chunk_cols,            include_transpose = include_transpose, precision = precision,            threads = threads, verbose = verbose) }

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: Currently Rcpp_sp_read returns bare dgCMatrix without dimnames even ROWNAMES/COLNAMES metadata present file. Fix R level: st_read (st_slice_cols, st_slice_rows, st_slice), calling Rcpp function: requires Rcpp_sp_metadata return rownames colnames list. Check already ; , extend bridge function include .
code: st_read <- function(path, cols = NULL, threads = 0L, reorder = TRUE) {   path <- normalizePath(path, mustWork = TRUE)   A <- Rcpp_sp_read(path, cols = cols, reorder = reorder, threads = as.integer(threads))   # Wire dimnames from metadata   info <- Rcpp_sp_metadata(path)   if (!is.null(info$rownames) && length(info$rownames) == nrow(A))     rownames(A) <- info$rownames   if (!is.null(info$colnames)) {     cn <- info$colnames     if (!is.null(cols)) cn <- cn[cols]     if (length(cn) == ncol(A)) colnames(A) <- cn   }   A }

code: namespace streampress {  // Write a sparse matrix (CSC format) to .spz file // rows, cols, nnz: matrix dimensions // col_ptr: CSC column pointers (length cols+1) // row_ind: CSC row indices (length nnz) // values:  non-zero values (length nnz, float32) // obs_buf, var_buf: serialized obs/var table blobs (empty = no table) // options: write options struct WriteStats write_sparse(     const std::string& path,     uint32_t rows, uint32_t cols, uint64_t nnz,     const uint32_t* col_ptr,     const uint32_t* row_ind,     const float*    values,     const std::vector<uint8_t>& obs_buf = {},     const std::vector<uint8_t>& var_buf = {},     const WriteOptions& opts = WriteOptions{});  // Read full matrix ReadResult read_sparse(const std::string& path,                        const ReadOptions& opts = ReadOptions{});  // Read a contiguous range of columns ReadResult slice_cols(const std::string& path,                       uint32_t col_start, uint32_t col_end,                       const ReadOptions& opts = ReadOptions{});  // Read a set of rows (requires transpose section) ReadResult slice_rows(const std::string& path,                       const std::vector<uint32_t>& row_indices,                       const ReadOptions& opts = ReadOptions{});  // Get file info without decompression FileInfo info(const std::string& path);  // Chunk iterator (C++11 range-for compatible) class ChunkIterator { ... };  // see chunk_iterator.hpp  // Obs/var tables std::vector<uint8_t> make_obs_var_table(     const std::vector<std::string>& col_names,     ...);  // see obs_var_table.hpp  // Options struct WriteOptions {     bool    include_transpose = true;     bool    use_delta         = true;     bool    value_pred        = false;     bool    row_sort          = false;     uint32_t chunk_cols       = 0;     // 0 = compute from chunk_bytes     uint64_t chunk_bytes      = 64ULL * 1024 * 1024;  // 64 MB     uint32_t transp_chunk_cols = 0;    // 0 = compute from chunk_bytes     int     threads           = 0;     // 0 = all     std::string precision     = "auto"; };  struct ReadOptions {     bool reorder  = true;     int  threads  = 0; };  struct ReadResult {     std::vector<uint32_t> col_ptr;  // length n+1     std::vector<uint32_t> row_ind;     std::vector<float>    values;     uint32_t m, n;     uint64_t nnz;     std::vector<std::string> rownames, colnames; };  struct FileInfo {     uint32_t m, n;     uint64_t nnz;     uint32_t chunk_cols;     uint32_t transp_chunk_cols;     bool     has_transpose;     bool     has_obs_table;     bool     has_var_table;     bool     has_dimnames;     float    density;     uint64_t file_bytes;     uint16_t version; };  } // namespace streampress

title: WORKQUEUE: StreamPress (.spz) Full API Overhaul
text: Create benchmarks/harness/streampress/bench_streampress.R:
code: #!/usr/bin/env Rscript # StreamPress performance benchmark # Usage: Rscript bench_streampress.R [path.spz] # If no path given, generates synthetic data.  library(RcppML) library(Matrix) library(microbenchmark)  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE) if (length(args) >= 1) {   message("Loading real GEO file: ", args[1])   A <- st_read(args[1]) } else {   message("No file given — using synthetic 50k×10k, density=1%")   set.seed(42)   A <- rsparsematrix(50000, 10000, density = 0.01) } message(sprintf("Matrix: %d x %d, nnz=%d (%.2f%%)",         nrow(A), ncol(A), nnz(A), 100*nnz(A)/(nrow(A)*ncol(A))))  f <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz") on.exit(unlink(f))  # --- Write benchmarks ------------------------------------------------------- bw <- microbenchmark(   write_t1   = st_write(A, f, threads=1,  include_transpose=TRUE, verbose=FALSE),   write_t4   = st_write(A, f, threads=4,  include_transpose=TRUE, verbose=FALSE),   write_tall  = st_write(A, f, threads=0,  include_transpose=TRUE, verbose=FALSE),   times = 3 ) print(bw)  # Ensure file written for read benchmarks st_write(A, f, threads=0, include_transpose=TRUE, verbose=FALSE)  # --- Read benchmarks -------------------------------------------------------- br <- microbenchmark(   read_t1   = st_read(f, threads=1),   read_t4   = st_read(f, threads=4),   read_tall  = st_read(f, threads=0),   times = 3 ) print(br)  # --- Slice benchmarks ------------------------------------------------------- n_cols <- ncol(A) n_rows <- nrow(A) col_range <- 1:min(1000, n_cols) row_range <- 1:min(1000, n_rows)  bs <- microbenchmark(   slice_cols_t1  = st_slice_cols(f, col_range, threads=1),   slice_cols_t4  = st_slice_cols(f, col_range, threads=4),   slice_rows_t1  = st_slice_rows(f, row_range, threads=1),   slice_rows_t4  = st_slice_rows(f, row_range, threads=4),   times = 3 ) print(bs)  # --- Chunk iteration -------------------------------------------------------- bm <- microbenchmark(   map_all_t4 = st_map_chunks(f, function(chunk, cs, ce) NULL, threads=4),   times = 2 ) print(bm)  # --- Correctness check ------------------------------------------------------- A2 <- st_read(f) stopifnot(all.equal(A, A2)) message("Round-trip correctness: PASS")  # --- Speedup summary -------------------------------------------------------- message("\n=== Thread Speedup Summary ===") for (nm in c("write", "read", "slice_cols", "slice_rows")) {   t1  <- summary(get(paste0("b", substr(nm,1,1))))$mean[grepl(paste0(nm,"_t1"),  summary(get(paste0("b", substr(nm,1,1))))$expr)]   t4  <- summary(get(paste0("b", substr(nm,1,1))))$mean[grepl(paste0(nm,"_t4"),  summary(get(paste0("b", substr(nm,1,1))))$expr)]   message(sprintf("  %s: 1→4 threads = %.1fx speedup", nm, t1/t4)) }

library(RcppML)  # Load the current large dgCMatrix load("data/pbmc3k.rda")  # loads 'pbmc3k' as dgCMatrix original <- pbmc3k  # Write to StreamPress format tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz") st_write(original, tmp, precision = "fp32")  # fp32 is fine for count data  # Read back the SPZ bytes as raw vector pbmc3k <- readBin(tmp, "raw", n = file.info(tmp)$size)  # Save as .rda with gzip compression save(pbmc3k, file = "data/pbmc3k.rda", compress = "gzip")  # Verify file size cat("New .rda size:", file.info("data/pbmc3k.rda")$size / 1024, "KB
") cat("Old .rda would be ~2600 KB
")  # Verify round-trip load("data/pbmc3k.rda")  # loads raw vector tmp2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".spz") writeBin(pbmc3k, tmp2) recovered <- st_read(tmp2) cat("Round-trip OK:", all.equal(as.matrix(original), as.matrix(recovered)), "
")

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: NMF inherently soft clustering method. column H gives sample’s “membership” across k factors, taking argmax rows yields hard cluster assignment. However, NMF non-convex — different random initializations produce different solutions potentially different clusters. Consensus NMF addresses running many replicates, tracking samples consistently co-cluster, building robust consensus matrix. Beyond flat clustering, RcppML provides divisive hierarchical clustering via recursive rank-2 NMF splits, classification embeddings leverages low-dimensional H representation supervised tasks.

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: data — input matrix (features × samples) .spz path k — factorization rank (number clusters) reps — number NMF replicates (= stable; use ≤ 30 speed) method — "hard" assigns clusters dominant factor; "knn_jaccard" uses k-NN Jaccard overlap (robust ambiguity) Returns consensus_nmf object $consensus (n × n co-clustering matrix), $clusters (assignments), $cophenetic (stability metric), $models (fitted models). Use plot() consensus heatmap summary() cluster statistics.
code: consensus_nmf(data, k, reps = 50, method = c("hard", "knn_jaccard"),               knn = 10, seed = NULL, threads = 0, verbose = FALSE, ...)

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: bipartition() performs single rank-2 NMF split. dclust() recursively bipartitions clusters smaller min_samples modularity min_dist. Important: dclust() returns 0-indexed $samples. Add 1 R-style indexing.
code: bipartition(data, tol = 1e-5, nonneg = TRUE, ...) dclust(A, min_samples, min_dist = 0, tol = 1e-5, maxit = 100,        nonneg = TRUE, seed = NULL, threads = 0, verbose = FALSE)

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: Hungarian algorithm optimal 1:1 factor correspondence given cost matrix. Returns 0-indexed $assignment total $cost.
code: bipartiteMatch(x)

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: take samples × features embedding matrix (e.g., t(model@h)) class labels. Return fn_classifier_eval object $accuracy, $macro_f1, $confusion, per-class metrics.
code: classify_embedding(embedding, labels, test_fraction = 0.2, k = 5L, seed = NULL) classify_logistic(embedding, labels, test_fraction = 0.2, seed = NULL)

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: consensus matrix CC defined element-wise: CijC_{ij} fraction replicates samples ii jj assigned cluster. Perfect clustering produces binary consensus (0 1); noisy unstable clustering yields intermediate values. cophenetic correlation measures well hierarchical clustering consensus matrix preserves pairwise distances. Higher values indicate stable clustering. Divisive clustering recursively applies rank-2 NMF. split finds best binary partition; process stops clusters small homogeneous (low modularity). Classification H: Columns H k-dimensional embeddings samples. classifier (k-NN, logistic regression) applied embeddings can separate classes, assuming captured NMF factors.

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: Golub leukemia dataset contains 38 bone marrow samples — 27 11 AML — measured across 5,000 genes. Can unsupervised NMF discover known subtypes?  consensus heatmap reveals two tightly co-clustered groups. Let’s check well correspond known /AML labels. Consensus clusters vs. true cancer type Consensus NMF summary statistics Consensus NMF k = 2 cleanly separates two leukemia subtypes. cophenetic correlation confirms high clustering stability across replicates.
code: data(golub) labels <- attr(golub, "cancer_type")  # Consensus NMF: samples (rows) x genes (columns) cons <- consensus_nmf(golub, k = 2, reps = 20, seed = 42, verbose = FALSE) plot(cons, show_clusters = TRUE) +   ggtitle("Consensus matrix: Golub leukemia (k = 2, 20 replicates)") # Confusion matrix: consensus clusters vs. true labels conf <- table(Cluster = cons$clusters, Cancer = labels) knitr::kable(conf, caption = "Consensus clusters vs. true cancer type") summary_df <- data.frame(   Metric = c("Cophenetic correlation", "Cluster 1 size", "Cluster 2 size"),   Value = c(round(cons$cophenetic, 4),             sum(cons$clusters == 1),             sum(cons$clusters == 2)) ) knitr::kable(summary_df, caption = "Consensus NMF summary statistics")

what: Example 2: Hierarchical Clustering with dclust (AML Chromatin)
title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: AML dataset contains 824 chromatin accessibility regions across 135 samples known subtype annotations. Divisive clustering discovers hierarchy subtypes. Divisive clustering AML chromatin data Divisive clustering discovers hierarchy subtypes, early splits separating biologically distinct cell populations. cluster’s composition reflects known AML subtype structure.
code: data(aml) meta <- attr(aml, "metadata_h")  clusters <- dclust(aml, min_samples = 10, min_dist = 0.01, seed = 42)  # Build cluster composition table cluster_info <- do.call(rbind, lapply(clusters, function(cl) {   sample_idx <- cl$samples + 1L  # 0-indexed to 1-indexed   categories <- meta$category[sample_idx]   top_cats <- sort(table(categories), decreasing = TRUE)   data.frame(     Cluster = cl$id,     Size = cl$size,     `Top Category` = names(top_cats)[1],     `Top Count` = as.integer(top_cats[1]),     `Composition` = paste(paste0(names(top_cats)[1:min(3, length(top_cats))], ":",                                   as.integer(top_cats[1:min(3, length(top_cats))])),                           collapse = ", "),     check.names = FALSE   ) }))  knitr::kable(head(cluster_info, 8), caption = "Divisive clustering of AML chromatin data")

title: Clustering, Consensus, and Classification
text: Using Golub NMF model, compare classification accuracy k-NN logistic regression applied H embedding. Classification NMF embeddings (k = 3) classifiers achieve high accuracy just 3 NMF factors, confirming low-dimensional embedding captures /AML distinction effectively. NMF representation compresses 5,000 genes handful components without losing discriminative power.
code: # Fit NMF to get embeddings model <- nmf(t(golub), k = 3, seed = 42, maxit = 100) embedding <- t(model@h)  # 38 samples x 3 factors int_labels <- as.integer(labels) - 1L  # 0-indexed  # Use same test split for fair comparison set.seed(42) n <- nrow(embedding) test_idx <- sort(sample(n, floor(n * 0.3)))  knn_eval <- classify_embedding(embedding, int_labels, test_idx = test_idx, k = 3, seed = 42) log_eval <- classify_logistic(embedding, int_labels, test_idx = test_idx, seed = 42)  comp_table <- data.frame(   Method = c("k-NN (k=3)", "Logistic regression"),   Accuracy = c(knn_eval$accuracy, log_eval$accuracy),   `Macro F1` = c(knn_eval$macro_f1, log_eval$macro_f1),   check.names = FALSE ) knitr::kable(comp_table, digits = 3,              caption = "Classification from NMF embeddings (k = 3)")
