title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: Project factor matrix onto new data solve complementary matrix. Given \(W\) \(\), find \(H\) \(||WH - ||^2\) minimized. Alternatively, given \(H\) \(\), find \(W\) \(||WH - ||^2\) minimized.

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
code: nnls(   w = NULL,   h = NULL,   A,   warm_start = NULL,   L1 = c(0, 0),   L2 = c(0, 0),   cd_maxit = 100L,   cd_tol = 1e-08,   upper_bound = c(0, 0),   nonneg = c(TRUE, TRUE),   threads = 0,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: w factor model matrix (n_features x k) solving H. Set NULL solve W. h factor model matrix (k x n_samples) solving W. Set NULL solve H. data matrix (n_features x n_samples w->h, n_samples x n_features h->w). Can dense (matrix) sparse (dgCMatrix Matrix package). warm_start optional matrix initialize solution. Must match output dimensions (k x n_samples H, n_features x k W). Can accelerate convergence. L1 L1/LASSO penalty, length 1 2 c(w_penalty, h_penalty). Range [0, 1). L2 Ridge penalty, length 1 2 c(w_penalty, h_penalty). Range [0, Inf). cd_maxit maximum number coordinate descent iterations (default 100) cd_tol stopping tolerance coordinate descent (default 1e-8) upper_bound maximum value solution, length 1 2 c(w_bound, h_bound). 0 = bound. nonneg non-negativity constraints, length 1 2 c(w_constraint, h_constraint) threads number threads OpenMP parallelization (0 = available) verbose print progress information

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: matrix dimension (k x n_samples) H (n_features x k) W

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: function solves NNLS projection problems full flexibility: Projection Modes: w provided, h = NULL: Solve H given W (standard projection) h provided, w = NULL: Solve W given H (transpose projection) Exactly one w h must NULL Normal Equations: H: \(W'W H = W'\) W: \(HH' W' = HA'\) (solved transpose problem) problem solved forming Gram matrix right-hand side C++ OpenMP parallelization across samples. Sequential coordinate descent warm starts enables fast convergence. Dimension Matching: dimensions match exactly, function attempts : Auto-transpose one orientation matches Match rownames/colnames reorder/subset intersection Error valid dimension alignment possible Warm Start: Provide optional warm_start matrix initialize solution. can dramatically accelerate convergence solutions expected similar across related problems (e.g., incremental updates, time series). Penalties: L1 (LASSO): Encourages sparsity subtracting L1 right-hand side L2 (Ridge): Shrinks solutions adding L2 diagonal Gram matrix Graph Laplacian: yet implemented nnls. Use nmf() graph regularization. Length-2 vectors c(w_penalty, h_penalty) control penalties W H separately

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: nnls() uses data matrix (linear algebra convention), matching svd() normal equations \(W'WH = W'\). contrast, nmf() uses data (statistical modeling convention).

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: DeBruine, ZJ, Melcher, K, Triche, TJ. (2021). "High-performance non-negative matrix factorization large single-cell data." BioRXiv. Franc, VC, Hlavac, VC, Navara, M. (2005). "Sequential Coordinate-Wise Algorithm Non-negative Least Squares Problem."

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
text: Zach DeBruine

title: Non-negative Least Squares Projection — nnls
code: # \donttest{ # Generate synthetic NMF problem set.seed(123) w <- matrix(runif(100), 20, 5)  # 20 features, 5 factors h_true <- matrix(runif(50), 5, 10)  # 5 factors, 10 samples A <- w %*% h_true + matrix(rnorm(200, 0, 0.1), 20, 10)  # Add noise  # Project W onto new data to find H h_recovered <- nnls(w = w, A = A) cor(as.vector(h_true), as.vector(h_recovered)) #> [1] 0.9668001  # Project H onto new features to find W (transpose problem) w_recovered <- nnls(h = h_true, A = A) cor(as.vector(w), as.vector(w_recovered)) #> [1] 0.8989978  # With L1 penalty for sparse H h_sparse <- nnls(w = w, A = A, L1 = c(0, 0.1))  # Warm start for faster convergence h_init <- matrix(0.5, 5, 10) h_warm <- nnls(w = w, A = A, warm_start = h_init)  # Allow negative values (semi-NMF) h_unconstrained <- nnls(w = w, A = A, nonneg = c(TRUE, FALSE))  # Dimension matching by names rownames(w) <- paste0("gene_", 1:20) rownames(A) <- paste0("gene_", 20:1)  # Reversed order h_matched <- nnls(w = w, A = A)  # Automatically reorders A by rownames # }

title: Random transpose-identical dense/sparse matrix — r_matrix
text: nrow number rows ncol number columns transpose_identical matrix transpose-identical? inv_density integer giving inverse density matrix (.e. 10 percent density corresponds inv_density = 10). Density probabilistic, exact. See examples. pattern pattern matrix (Matrix::ngCMatrix) returned? , Matrix::dgCMatrix random uniform values returned.

title: Random transpose-identical dense/sparse matrix — r_matrix
text: dense numeric matrix dimensions nrow ncol random uniform values. sparse matrix: ngCMatrix pattern = TRUE, otherwise dgCMatrix.

title: Random transpose-identical dense/sparse matrix — r_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # generate a simple random matrix A <- r_matrix(10, 10)  # generate two matrices that are transpose identical set.seed(123) A1 <- r_matrix(10, 100, transpose_identical = TRUE) set.seed(123) A2 <- r_matrix(100, 10, transpose_identical = TRUE) all.equal(t(A2), A1)  # generate a transpose-identical pair of speckled matrices set.seed(123) A <- r_sparsematrix(10, 100, inv_density = 10, transpose_identical = TRUE) set.seed(123) A <- r_sparsematrix(100, 10, inv_density = 10, transpose_identical = TRUE) all.equal(t(A), A) Matrix::isSymmetric(A[1:10, 1:10]) heatmap(as.matrix(A), scale = "none", Rowv = NA, Colv = NA)  # note that density is probabilistic, not absolute A <- replicate(1000, r_sparsematrix(100, 100, 10)) densities <- sapply(A, function(x) length(x@i) / prod(dim(x))) plot(density(densities)) # normal distribution centered at 0.100 range(densities) } # }

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
text: r_sample just like base::sample, faster. r_sample takes sample specified size elements x using replacement indicated. functions generate random distributions (uniform, normal, binomial) just like base R counterparts (runif, rnorm, rbinom), faster.

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
code: r_sample(x, size = NULL, replace = FALSE)  r_unif(n, min = 0, max = 1)  r_binom(n, size = 1, inv_prob = 2)

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
text: x either positive integer giving number items choose , vector elements shuffle choose. See 'Details'. size number trials (one ) replace sampling replacement? n number observations min finite lower limit uniform distribution max finite upper limit uniform distribution inv_prob inverse probability success trial, must integral (e.g. 50 percent success = 2, 10 percent success = 10)

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
text: r_sample: vector sampled elements x. r_unif: numeric vector random values uniform distribution. r_binom: numeric vector random values binomial distribution.

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
text: RNGs make use Marsaglia's xorshift method generate random integers. r_unif takes random integer divides seed returns floating decimal portion result.

title: Random distributions and samples — r_sample
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # draw all integers from 1 to 10 in a random order r_sample(10)  # shuffle a vector of values v <- r_unif(3) v v_ <- r_sample(v) v_  # draw values from a vector r_sample(r_unif(100), 3)  # draw some integers between 1 and 1000 r_sample(1000, 3) } # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # simulate a uniform distribution v <- r_unif(10000) plot(density(v))  # simulate a binomial distribution v <- r_binom(10000, 100, inv_prob = 10) hist(v) sum(v) / length(v) # ~10 because 100 trials at 10 percent success odds #   is about 10 successes per element  # get successful trials in a bernoulli distribution v <- r_binom(100, 1, 20) successful_trials <- slot(as(v, "nsparseVector"), "i") successful_trials } # }

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
text: Given baseline MSE-fitted NMF model original data, computes score-test statistics power-variance family (\(V(\mu) = \mu^p\)) determine best-fitting distribution without refitting. Optionally tests NB overdispersion.

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
code: score_test_distribution(   data,   model,   powers = c(0, 1, 2, 3),   test_nb = TRUE,   min_mu = 1e-06 )

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
text: data Original data matrix (sparse dense) model fitted NMF object (nmf() loss="mse") powers Numeric vector variance powers test. Default c(0, 1, 2, 3) covers Gaussian, Poisson, Gamma, Inverse Gaussian. test_nb Logical; TRUE data integer-valued, also test NB overdispersion diagnostic. min_mu Floor predicted values avoid division zero. Default 1e-6.

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
text: list : scores Data frame columns power, T_stat, abs_T,     distribution (label) best_power Numeric: power p smallest |T_p| best_distribution Character: name best-matching distribution nb_diagnostic test_nb=TRUE: list T_NB     overdispersed (logical)

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
text: score test statistic variance power \(p\) : $$T_p = \text{mean}\left(\frac{r_{ij}^2}{\mu_{ij}^p} - 1\right)$$ \(r_{ij} = x_{ij} - \mu_{ij}\) residuals \(\mu_{ij} = (WH)_{ij}\) predicted values. correct model, \(E[T_p] = 0\). power minimizing \(|T_p|\) best matches observed variance-mean relationship. NB diagnostic tests quadratic overdispersion: $$T_{NB} = \text{mean}\left(\frac{r_{ij}^2 - \mu_{ij}}{\mu_{ij}^2}\right)$$ \(T_{NB} > 0.1\), substantial overdispersion beyond Poisson, suggesting NB may preferable GP.

title: Score-test distribution diagnostic — score_test_distribution
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ A <- abs(rsparsematrix(200, 100, 0.3)) model <- nmf(A, k = 5, loss = "mse") diag <- score_test_distribution(A, model) print(diag$scores) cat("Best distribution:", diag$best_distribution, "\n") } # }

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
text: Generate synthetic "swimmer" dataset consisting stick figure images various swimming poses. classic benchmark dataset NMF, originally described Donoho Stodden (2003). image 32x32 pixel representation stick figure torso four limbs (left arm, right arm, left leg, right leg), limb can one 4 positions.

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
code: simulateSwimmer(   n_images = 256,   style = c("stick", "gaussian"),   sigma = 1.5,   noise = 0,   seed = NULL,   return_factors = FALSE )

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
text: n_images number images generate (default: 256 combinations) style either "stick" stick figures "gaussian" smoothed Gaussian blobs (default: "stick") sigma standard deviation Gaussian smoothing style = "gaussian" (default: 1.5) noise standard deviation Gaussian noise add (default: 0) seed seed random number generation return_factors logical, TRUE return true W H factors (default: FALSE)

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
text: return_factors = FALSE, returns sparse matrix (n_images x 1024)   row flattened 32x32 image. return_factors = TRUE, returns   list components: data matrix (n_images x 1024) w True factor matrix W (n_images x 16) h True factor matrix H (16 x 1024) limb_positions Matrix (n_images x 4) indicating limb positions

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
text: swimmer dataset consists stick figure images : fixed torso (circle head, vertical line body) 4 limbs, 4 possible angles: 0, 45, 90, 135 degrees body Total 16 "limb factors" (4 limbs x 4 positions ) 256 possible combinations sampling positions true rank dataset 16 (number unique limb positions). return_factors = TRUE, function returns true generative factors W (256 x 16) H (16 x 1024), column H represents one limb position pattern. Style options: style = "stick": Sharp lines limbs (binary image) style = "gaussian": Smoothed Gaussian kernel softer edges

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
text: Donoho, D. Stodden, V. (2003). "non-negative matrix factorization give correct decomposition parts?" Advances Neural Information Processing Systems 16.

title: Simulate Swimmer Dataset — simulateSwimmer
code: # \donttest{ # Generate all 256 swimmer combinations swimmers <- simulateSwimmer() dim(swimmers)  # 256 x 1024 #> [1]  256 1024  # Generate random subset with Gaussian smoothing swimmers_smooth <- simulateSwimmer(n_images = 100, style = "gaussian", seed = 123)  # Get true factors for validation swimmer_data <- simulateSwimmer(return_factors = TRUE) # Run NMF and compare to true factors model <- nmf(swimmer_data$A, k = 16, maxit = 100) # }

what: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
code: solve(   a,   b,   cd_maxit = 100L,   cd_tol = 1e-08,   L1 = 0,   L2 = 0,   upper_bound = 0,   nonneg = TRUE )

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: symmetric positive definite matrix giving coefficients linear system b matrix giving right-hand side(s) linear system cd_maxit maximum number coordinate descent iterations cd_tol stopping criteria, difference \(x\) across consecutive solutions sum \(x\) L1 L1/LASSO penalty subtracted b L2 Ridge penalty added diagonal upper_bound maximum value permitted solution, set 0 impose upper bound nonneg TRUE (default), impose non-negativity constraint solution

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: vector matrix giving solution x

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: fast implementation sequential coordinate descent least squares, suitable small large systems. algorithm begins zero-filled initialization x. Least squares sequential coordinate descent used ensure solution returned exact. algorithm introduced Franc et al. (2005), implementation vectorized optimized rendition found NNLM R package Xihui Lin (2020). nonneg = TRUE, solves Non-Negative Least Squares (NNLS) problem. nonneg = FALSE, solves unconstrained least squares.

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: function deprecated. Use nnls instead. solve() previously masked base::solve().

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: DeBruine, ZJ, Melcher, K, Triche, TJ. (2021). "High-performance non-negative matrix factorization large single-cell data." BioRXiv. Franc, VC, Hlavac, VC, Navara, M. (2005). "Sequential Coordinate-Wise Algorithm Non-negative Least Squares Problem. Proc. Int'l Conf. Computer Analysis Images Patterns." Lin, X, Boutros, PC (2020). "Optimization expansion non-negative matrix factorization." BMC Bioinformatics.

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
text: Zach DeBruine

title: Solve a linear system with coordinate descent — solve
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # compare solution to base::solve for a random system X <- matrix(runif(100), 10, 10) a <- crossprod(X) b <- crossprod(X, runif(10))  # unconstrained solution (same as base::solve) unconstrained_soln <- solve(a, b, nonneg = FALSE)  # non-negative constrained solution nonneg_soln <- solve(a, b, nonneg = TRUE) } # }

title: Read SparsePress File Directly to GPU Memory — sp_read_gpu

title: Read SparsePress File Directly to GPU Memory — sp_read_gpu

title: Write a sparse matrix to a StreamPress file — st_write
text: x sparse matrix (dgCMatrix) object coercible one. path Output file path. Extension .spz recommended. obs Optional data.frame observation (row/cell) metadata. Must nrow(obs) == nrow(x). var Optional data.frame variable (column/gene) metadata. Must nrow(var) == ncol(x). delta Logical; use density-based delta prediction structure. Default TRUE. value_pred Logical; use value prediction integer-valued data. Default FALSE. verbose Logical; print compression statistics. Default FALSE. precision Value precision: "auto" (default), "fp32", "fp16", "quant8", "fp64". row_sort Logical; sort rows nnz better compression. include_transpose Logical; store CSC(^T) file. Default TRUE. chunk_cols Integer NULL; columns per chunk. NULL, computed chunk_bytes. chunk_bytes Numeric; target bytes per chunk chunk_cols NULL. Default 64 MB. transp_chunk_cols Integer NULL; columns per transpose chunk. transp_chunk_bytes Numeric NULL; target bytes per transpose chunk. threads Integer; number threads (0 = available). Default 0.

title: Write a sparse matrix to a StreamPress file — st_write
text: Invisibly returns list compression statistics.

what: StreamPress I/O: Read, Write, and Inspect Compressed Matrices — streampress
title: StreamPress I/O: Read, Write, and Inspect Compressed Matrices — streampress
text: Functions reading writing matrices StreamPress (.spz) format. StreamPress (formerly SparsePress) achieves 10-20x compression typical scRNA-seq sparse matrices using rANS entropy coding, supports dense v3 format multiple compression codecs.

title: StreamPress I/O: Read, Write, and Inspect Compressed Matrices — streampress
text: StreamPress (.spz) supports two format versions: v2 (sparse): Column-oriented compressed CSC format.     Lossless, 10-20x compression. Self-describing header. v3 (dense): Column-major dense panels optional     FP16/QUANT8/rANS compression. streaming NMF dense data.

what: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: Compute truncated SVD PCA matrix using multiple algorithm backends. Supports sparse dense matrices, implicit PCA centering, per-element regularization (L1/L2/non-negativity/bounds), Gram-level regularization (L2,1/angular/graph Laplacian), automatic rank selection via speckled holdout cross-validation.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
code: svd(   A,   k = 10,   tol = 1e-05,   maxit = 200,   center = FALSE,   scale = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   threads = 0,   L1 = 0,   L2 = 0,   nonneg = FALSE,   upper_bound = 0,   L21 = 0,   angular = 0,   graph_U = NULL,   graph_V = NULL,   graph_lambda = 0,   convergence = "factor",   test_fraction = 0,   cv_seed = NULL,   patience = 3,   mask_zeros = FALSE,   obs_mask = NULL,   robust = FALSE,   k_max = 50,   resource = "auto",   method = "auto" )  sparse_pca(A, k = 10, L1 = 0.5, ...)  nn_pca(A, k = 10, ...)  svd_pca(...)

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: Input matrix. May dense (matrix), sparse (dgCMatrix), path .spz file --core streaming SVD. k Number factors (rank). Use "auto" automatic rank selection via cross-validation. Default: 10. tol Convergence tolerance per rank-1 subproblem. Measures \(1 - |\cos(u_{new}, u_{old})|\). Default: 1e-5. maxit Maximum ALS iterations per factor. Default: 200. center TRUE, subtract row means (PCA mode). Default: FALSE. scale TRUE, divide row standard deviation centering (correlation PCA mode). Implies center = TRUE. computes PCA correlation matrix rather covariance matrix. Default: FALSE. verbose Print per-factor diagnostics. Default: FALSE. seed Random seed initialization cross-validation. Default: NULL (random). Use integer reproducibility. threads Number OpenMP threads. 0 = available. Default: 0. L1 L1 (lasso) penalty. Either single value (applied u v) length-2 vector c(L1_u, L1_v). Default: 0. L2 L2 (ridge) penalty. Either single value length-2 vector. Default: 0. nonneg Non-negativity constraints. Either single logical (sides) length-2 logical vector c(nonneg_u, nonneg_v). Default: FALSE. upper_bound Upper bound constraints. Single value length-2 vector. Default: 0 (bound). L21 L2,1 (group sparsity) penalty. Drives entire components toward zero. Single value length-2 vector c(L21_u, L21_v). Default: 0. Supported : deflation (adaptive L2), krylov (Gram-level). angular Angular (orthogonality) penalty. Decorrelates components. Single value length-2 vector c(angular_u, angular_v). Default: 0. Supported : deflation krylov. graph_U Sparse graph Laplacian matrix features (rows, m x m). Encourages smooth loadings along feature graph edges. Default: NULL. Supported : deflation krylov. graph_V Sparse graph Laplacian matrix samples (columns, n x n). Default: NULL. Supported : deflation krylov. graph_lambda Graph regularization strength. Single value length-2 vector c(graph_u_lambda, graph_v_lambda). Default: 0. convergence Convergence criterion: "factor" (track change factors, default), "loss" (track change explained variance), "" (stop either criterion met). test_fraction Fraction entries hold cross-validation / auto-rank. Default: 0 (disabled). Set value (0, 1) enable CV holdout, use k = "auto" automatically sets 0.05. cv_seed Separate seed holdout mask. Default: NULL (derive seed). patience auto-rank: stop many non-improving factors. Default: 3. mask_zeros TRUE, non-zero entries can holdout (sparse recommendation data). Default: FALSE. obs_mask Optional sparse matrix (dgCMatrix) dimensions . Non-zero entries indicate observations exclude fitting (e.g., known outliers missing-data indicators). Currently supported deflation method. Default: NULL (masking). robust Robustness control outlier downweighting via Huber loss. FALSE (default): standard MSE (robustness). TRUE: Huber-type robustness delta=1.345 (95% asymptotic efficiency). "mae": near-MAE behavior (delta=1e-4). Numeric: custom Huber delta value. Uses IRLS reweighting within ALS deflation loop. Supported deflation method ; methods automatically redirected deflation robust active. k_max Maximum rank auto-rank mode. Default: 50. resource Compute backend: "auto" (default, auto-detect), "cpu", "gpu". method Algorithm use. One "auto" (default), "deflation", "krylov", "lanczos", "irlba", "randomized". "auto": constraints route deflation/krylov; otherwise method selected based rank resource (GPU: Lanczos k<32, Randomized 32<=k<64, IRLBA k>=64; CPU: Lanczos k<64, IRLBA k>=64). ... Additional arguments passed svd (used convenience wrappers).

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: S4 object class svd_pca slots: u Left singular vectors (scores), m x k matrix d Singular values, length-k numeric vector v Right singular vectors (loadings), n x k matrix misc List metadata: centered, row_means,     test_loss, iters_per_factor, wall_time_ms

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: Multiple algorithms available via method parameter: "deflation" Sequential rank-1 ALS deflation correction.     Supports constraints/CV/auto-rank. Best small k mixed constraints. "krylov" Krylov-Seeded Projected Refinement (KSPR). Block     method: computes k factors simultaneously via Lanczos seed + Gram-solve--project.     Faster deflation larger k. Supports regularization. CV     evaluated post-convergence. "lanczos" Unconstrained Lanczos bidiagonalization. Fast     unconstrained SVD. regularization support. "irlba" Implicitly Restarted Lanczos. Good general-purpose     unconstrained SVD. regularization support. "randomized" Randomized SVD power iterations. Fast     approximate SVD large matrices. regularization support.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: function shadows base::svd(). use base R version, call base::svd() explicitly. RcppML version provides iterative SVD algorithms suitable large sparse matrices, whereas base::svd() computes full SVD using LAPACK.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: k = "auto", speckled holdout set created test MSE evaluated factor. Rank selection stops test MSE fails improve patience consecutive factors. returned model truncated best rank.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: pca(, k, ...): PCA (svd(, k, center = TRUE, ...)) sparse_pca(, k, L1, ...): PCA L1 sparsity v nn_pca(, k, ...): Non-negative PCA (centered, non-negative u v) svd_pca(...): Deprecated alias svd()

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: Robust + non-deflation method Huber-type robustness (robust=TRUE     numeric delta) requires IRLS reweighting supported     deflation method. methods automatically redirected     deflation robust active. GPU dense streaming GPU streaming SVD dense matrices yet     implemented. Dense streaming requires SPZ v3 dense format support. GPU Krylov dense Krylov method GPU dense     implementation. Use lanczos, irlba, randomized     GPU dense input. Constraints + matrix-free methods Regularization (L1, L2, L21, angular,     graph, nonneg, upper_bound) supported deflation     krylov methods. Using constraints lanczos, irlba,     randomized produce error.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: Several parameters svd() differ intentionally nmf(): Input naming svd() uses (linear algebra convention);     nmf() uses data (statistical modeling convention). Graph parameters svd() uses graph_U/graph_V     matching SVD factor names (U, V); nmf() uses     graph_W/graph_H. Penalty shape svd() accepts scalars (expanded internally     length-2); nmf() requires explicit c(w, h) vectors.     scalar API simpler per-factor SVD iteration. Non-negativity shape svd() accepts single logical;     nmf() requires c(w, h). SVD rarely needs     side-specific control. Tolerance svd() defaults tol = 1e-5     (per-factor convergence); nmf() defaults tol = 1e-4     (global convergence across factors simultaneously).

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd
text: sparse_pca() deprecated. Use svd(center=TRUE, L1=c(0, L1)) instead. nn_pca() deprecated. Use svd(center=TRUE, nonneg=TRUE) instead. svd_pca() deprecated. Use svd() instead.

title: Truncated SVD / PCA with constraints and regularization — svd

title: RcppML 1.0.1
text: Fixed GCC template deduction failure nullptr passed const Scalar* IRLS parameters (4 call sites fit_cpu.hpp) Fixed examples using unexported internal functions (r_sparsematrix, mse) Fixed undocumented parameters (dispersion, theta_init) nmf() documentation Fixed tests referencing removed parameters (ortho, convergence_str, sparse, convergence, precision) Removed duplicate cv_irls_weight helper (shadowing compute_irls_weight fit_cpu.hpp) Removed duplicate detail::extract_scaling (shadowing variant::extract_scaling fit_cpu.hpp) Replaced 9 deprecated function pairs (separate sparse/dense variants) single unified templates, eliminating dead code paths

title: RcppML 1.0.1
text: Unified NMF configuration single NMFConfig struct (Phases 1-2) Internalized GPU dispatch R .C() bridge C++ gateway (Phase 3) Consolidated header directory structure: algorithms/nmf/ + gateway/ merged nmf/, algorithms/svd/ merged svd/ (Phases 5-7) Added FitHistory callback logging fit loops (Phase 8) Mechanical renames: ortho -> angular, splitmix64 -> rng (Phase 4) SVD initialization auto-selection: Lanczos k<32, IRLBA k>=32 Unified CPU data access layer: New DataAccessor<MatrixType> template (Phase 10) provides single code path sparse dense matrix access, replacing ~40% duplicated sparse/dense update loops NMF solver GPU streaming NMF: Added fit_gpu_streaming.cuh matrices large GPU VRAM; fit_chunked_gpu.cuh tiles computation panel_cols-wide column panels CPU W-update cache optimisation: Pre-computed W^T stored contiguous column-major buffer; avoids scattered column reads Gram matrix inner loop fit_cpu.hpp restructured (1,442 → 980 lines): Initialization helpers extracted nmf_init.hpp, gram-trick explicit loss functions explicit_loss.hpp, masked-NNLS code masked_nnls.hpp; three #include’d RcppML::nmf namespace fit_cv.hpp restructured (1,845 → 1,032 lines): CV mask generation, IRLS weight helpers, fold bookkeeping split focused translation units gpu_bridge.cu split (1,618 lines → 4 focused translation units): gpu_bridge_common.cuh (shared headers/helpers), gpu_bridge_cluster.cu (bipartition/dclust), gpu_bridge_nmf.cu (NMF variants), gpu_bridge_svd.cu (SVD/PCA), gpu_bridge_utils.cu (profiler); Makefile.gpu updated accordingly Development documentation consolidated docs/dev/ New tests/testthat/test_chunked_gpu.R: 5 GPU-specific tests (basic validity, loss convergence, reproducibility, chunked vs non-chunked consistency, panel-size sweep); skip gracefully CPU-nodes

title: RcppML 1.0.0 (2026-02-27)
text: Despite S3→S4 class migration breaking changes, backward compatibility maintained existing reverse dependencies: - S4 $ accessor override: Old code using model$w model$h continues work new S4 objects. $ method transparently accesses S4 slots. - S4 $<- setter override: Assignment using model$w <- value syntax remains functional. - Preserved function signatures: Core functions nmf() nnls() maintain original parameter names behaviors. - Maintained model structure: w, d, h, misc components remain accessible via $ (backward compatible) @ (S4 native) accessors. - changes common use cases: Standard workflows like model <- nmf(, k=5); w <- model$w work unchanged. ensures packages depending RcppML (GeneNMF, phytoclass, CARDspa, scater, miloR, flashier) continue function without modification.

title: RcppML 1.0.0 (2026-02-27)
text: "mse" (default): Mean Squared Error (Frobenius norm) "mae": Mean Absolute Error (L1 loss), robust outliers "huber": Huber loss, blend MSE MAE configurable huber_delta "kl": Kullback-Leibler divergence, suitable count data Non-MSE losses use Iteratively Reweighted Least Squares (IRLS) optimization. Unified graph regularization: New graph_lambda = c(w, h) parameter replaces separate lambda_W lambda_H parameters. Accepts single value (applies ) vector independent control. "random" (default): Random initialization Integer: Sets random seed reproducible initialization Matrix: Custom W initialization matrix (dimensions p × k) Flexible upper bounds: upper_bound parameter now accepts c(w, h) vector independent bounds W H matrices. Single value applies (backward compatible). New cv_folds parameter k-fold cross-validation (e.g., cv_folds = 5 5-fold CV) Renamed holdout_fraction cv_fraction clarity Auto-enables track_loss CV active CV disabled cv_folds = 0 cv_fraction = 0 (default) Unified NMF class: Merged nmf_sparse nmf_dense single templated nmf<Mat> class using constexpr compile-time dispatch based matrix type. reduces code duplication ~20% maintaining full backward compatibility type aliases. Pure C++ header library: headers inst/include/RcppML/ now completely Rcpp-free, using Eigen C++ standard library. makes core algorithms portable usable non-R C++ projects. Simplified sparse matrix handling: Removed custom RcppML::SparseMatrix RcppML::MappedSparseMatrix typedefs. Now uses Eigen::SparseMatrix<double> Eigen::Map<const Eigen::SparseMatrix<double>> directly throughout codebase. Consolidated header structure: Merged RcppMLCommon.hpp RcppML.h single entry point C++ library. Template consistency: Unified template infrastructure across NMF, NNLS, bipartition, clustering components consistent precision handling. core algorithms now support arbitrary scalar types via templates (currently using double precision). Better cross-validation, now exclusively using mean squared error missing value imputation (random speckled patterns missing values) Complete migration S4 system, backwards compatibility CRAN version 0.5.0 New vignettes built-datasets
